factors.summary: The company is not explicitly mentioned to be raising prices, holding, or discounting. The text does not provide direct evidence on pricing power.

evidence.text: There is no specific mention of pricing power or customer responses to price actions in the provided text.

factors.summary: The company is involved in various legal proceedings and environmental remediation activities, with a recorded liability of $539 million at March 31, 2018.

evidence.text: We are involved with various environmental remediation activities and have recorded a liability of $539 million at March 31, 2018. For additional information, see Note 10 to our Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to be higher in 2018 compared to 2017, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We expect capital expenditures in 2018 to be higher than 2017.

factors.summary: The company is increasing shareholder returns through share repurchases, with $3.0 billion spent on repurchasing 8.9 million shares in the first quarter.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2018, net borrowings increased to $1.3 billion from $0.8 billion in the same period in 2017. We repurchased 8.9 million shares totaling $3.0 billion through our open market share repurchase program.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, with net cash provided by operating activities increasing from $2.1 billion to $3.1 billion year-over-year.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $3.1 billion during the three months ended March 31, 2018, compared with $2.1 billion during the same period in 2017.

factors.summary: Overall demand appears to be accelerating, supported by increased commercial airplane deliveries and higher revenues across multiple segments.

evidence.text: Revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2018 increased by $1,421 million compared with the same period in 2017. Commercial Airplanes (BCA) revenues increased by $699 million primarily due to higher deliveries and mix. Defense, Space & Security (BDS) revenues increased by $650 million primarily due to international contract awards for fighters and C-17 aircraft as well as higher weapons revenue. Global Services (BGS) revenues increased by $290 million, primarily due to higher commercial services revenue and supply chain services to government customers, partially offset by lower C-17 support services revenue.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, as evidenced by lower research and development expenses and improved cost performance on airplane programs.

evidence.text: Research and development expense for the three months ended March 31, 2018 decreased by $72 million compared with the same period in 2017, primarily due to lower 777X spend.
evidence.subsection: Total Costs and Expenses (“Cost of Sales”)

factors.summary: Contract backlog is growing, with increases in both funded and unfunded backlog across segments, indicating strong contract wins.

evidence.text: The increase during the three months ended March 31, 2018 was primarily due to orders and funding in excess of deliveries. Unobligated backlog includes U.S. and non-U.S. government definitive contracts for which funding has not been authorized. The increase during the three months ended March 31, 2018 was primarily due to contract awards, partially offset by reclassifications to contractual backlog related to BDS and BGS contracts.

factors.summary: Major programs are generally on track, though there are some challenges, such as higher estimated costs associated with certification and flight testing for the KC-46A Tanker.

evidence.text: The EMD contract is currently in the certification and flight testing phases and we expect 18 fully operational aircraft to be delivered in 2018. During 2017, we recorded further reach-forward losses of $446 million primarily reflecting higher estimated costs associated with certification and incorporating changes into LRIP aircraft. In the first quarter of 2018, we recorded additional reach-forward losses of $81 million primarily reflecting higher estimated costs associated with certification and continued flight testing.

factors.summary: Labor relations appear stable, with no significant issues reported that would impact talent retention.

evidence.text: The deferred compensation expense of $29 million and $50 million for the three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017 reflect increases in broad market conditions and our stock price.

factors.summary: R&D investment is decreasing, particularly in the Commercial Airplanes segment, as spending on the 777X program has reduced.

evidence.text: Research and development expense for the three months ended March 31, 2018 decreased by $72 million compared with the same period in 2017, primarily due to lower 777X spend.
evidence.subsection: Total Costs and Expenses (“Cost of Sales”)

factors.summary: Trade tariffs on steel and aluminum imports have been imposed, which may impact supply chain costs, but no significant direct impact on revenue or margins has been reported yet.

evidence.text: During the first quarter of 2018, the U.S. Government imposed tariffs on steel and aluminum imports. Several major U.S. trading partners are currently exempt from these tariffs; we are monitoring the potential for any extra costs that may result from them.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is positive, with core operating margins improving from 8.5% to 10.7%, and overall operating margins expanding due to higher earnings and improved cost performance across key segments.

evidence.text: Core operating margins (non-GAAP) improved from 8.5% to 10.7%
evidence.subsection: Reconciliation of GAAP Measures to Non-GAAP Measures

evidence.text: Earnings from operations for the three months ended March 31, 2018 increased by $669 million compared with the same period in 2017, primarily due to higher earnings at BCA and BDS, partially offset by other unallocated items and eliminations. BCA's earnings increased $638 million primarily due to improved cost performance on airplane programs, higher deliveries and mix, and lower spending on research and development. BDS earnings increased $100 million primarily due to improved performance on satellite services, and international contract awards for fighters and C-17 aircraft.

factors.summary: Volume trends are positive, with unit volumes increasing across the Commercial Airplanes segment, supported by higher revenues and contract awards, though there is a slight decrease in total deliveries compared to the prior year period.

evidence.text: BGS total backlog increased from $19,605 million as of December 31, 2017 to $20,464 million at March 31, 2018, primarily due to current year contract awards, partially offset by revenue recognized on contracts awarded in prior years.

Commercial airplane deliveries, including intercompany deliveries, were as follows: 737 * 747 † 767 777 787 Total Deliveries during the first three months of 2018 132 (5) 2 4 12 34 184 Deliveries during the first three months of 2017 113 (5) 1 1 21 32 169

factors.summary: The order backlog is growing across multiple segments, with significant increases in both total and contractual backlogs. The Commercial Airplanes segment, in particular, has seen a notable rise in its backlog due to orders exceeding deliveries. Additionally, the overall backlog has been bolstered by current year contract awards, though some offset has occurred due to revenue recognized from prior year contracts.

evidence.text: Total backlog increased from $44,049 million at December 31, 2017 to $50,404 million at March 31, 2018 primarily due to current year contract awards, including Ground-based Midcourse Defense, weapons and fighters, partially offset by revenue recognized on contracts awarded in prior years.

BCA total backlog increased from $411,188 million as of December 31, 2017 to $415,377 million at March 31, 2018 primarily due to orders in excess of deliveries. The increase during the three months ended March 31, 2018 was primarily due to orders and funding in excess of deliveries.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing strong organic revenue growth, with both revenue and earnings from operations increasing significantly compared to the previous year. The growth is evident across all major segments, driven by factors such as higher deliveries, international contract awards, and increased services revenue.

evidence.text: BDS revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2018 increased by $650 million, or 13% compared with the same period in 2017, primarily due to international contract awards for fighters and C-17 aircraft as well as higher weapons revenue. BDS earnings from operations for the three months ended March 31, 2018 increased by $100 million, or 18% compared with the same period in 2017.

Revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2018 increased by $1,421 million compared with the same period in 2017. Commercial Airplanes (BCA) revenues increased by $699 million primarily due to higher deliveries and mix. Defense, Space & Security (BDS) revenues increased by $650 million primarily due to international contract awards for fighters and C-17 aircraft as well as higher weapons revenue. Global Services (BGS) revenues increased by $290 million, primarily due to higher commercial services revenue and supply chain services to government customers, partially offset by lower C-17 support services revenue.

factors.summary: The government defense budgets are growing, with increased funding from the Bipartisan Budget Act of 2018 and FY18 Omnibus spending bill. This growth is particularly benefiting the company's core program areas such as fighters and C-17 aircraft, leading to improved performance and increased earnings from operations.

evidence.text: BDS earnings from operations for the three months ended March 31, 2018 increased by $100 million, or 18% compared with the same period in 2017 primarily due to improved performance on satellite services, and international contract awards for fighters and C-17 aircraft.

The Bipartisan Budget Act of 2018, passed in February 2018, raised the 2011 Budget Control Act spending caps for fiscal years 2018 and 2019 (FY18 and FY19). In addition, the FY18 Omnibus spending bill signed into law on March 23, 2018 provides funding for the remainder of the fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: United States Government Defense Environment Overview
